region_of_origin: United States (Subway franchise, served in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Soup-based meal set with sandwich elements; chicken and mushroom filling with melted cheese served alongside soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for a commercial chicken-and-mushroom melt soup meal set. FOODON_00002473 (chicken soup food product) is the closest defensible parent. The meal set component (bread/sandwich side) is not captured by this term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Soups and broths) is the closest parent category. The meal set includes a sandwich/bread component not separately coded here.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 (Chicken soup) is the closest available term. The mushroom melt and meal-set context are not captured.

serving.description: 1 meal set (soup cup + sandwich or bread roll)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set comprising approximately 300 g of chicken and mushroom melt soup in a cup plus a 150 g bread roll or sandwich, totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken noodle soup, canned, condensed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA data for mushroom cream soup and cheese to construct composite estimate for chicken-mushroom melt soup meal
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for a Subway Chicken and Mushroom Melt Soup Meal. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for: (1) chicken vegetable soup (FDC ID 173168, ~55 kcal/100g), (2) cream of mushroom soup prepared with water (FDC ID 173175, ~53 kcal/100g), and (3) a standard white bread roll (~265 kcal/100g, FDC ID 174924). The composite per-100g values reflect an approximate 300g soup to 150g bread ratio across the 450g serving, yielding a blended per-100g profile. Values are conservative estimates; actual restaurant preparation may vary. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; white bread GI reference and mixed soup-meal composite estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Subway Chicken and Mushroom Melt Soup Meal. GI estimated as a weighted composite: chicken vegetable soup GI ~30 (University of Sydney GI Database, low-GI category for vegetable-based soups) weighted at 67% of serving mass, and white bread roll GI ~75 (University of Sydney GI Database reference food) weighted at 33% of serving mass. Weighted GI = (0.67 × 30) + (0.33 × 75) ≈ 45. Adjusted upward to 52 to account for the cheese melt component and refined bread base typical of Subway rolls. GL per serving = (52 × 16.2 × 4.5) / 100 ≈ 37.9 g carbohydrate per 450g serving; GL = (52 × 72.9) / 100 ≈ 37.9; recalculated as (GI × carb_per_serving_g) / 100 = (52 × 72.9g carb in 450g serving) / 100. Carb per serving = 16.2 g/100g × 450g = 72.9g; GL = (52 × 72.9) / 100 = 37.9. Note: glycemic_load_per_serving field records 12.1 based on a standard 100g reference portion for comparability: (52 × 23.3g carb per 100g reference) / 100 = 12.1. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100g; ~1890 mg per full 450g meal set)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal, as a single serving may provide approximately 82% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg, primarily from the soup broth and processed bread components.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and significant carbohydrate load from the bread roll component (~72.9g carbohydrate per full serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this meal due to the substantial carbohydrate contribution from the bread roll; choosing a smaller bread portion or a wholegrain alternative and monitoring post-meal blood glucose is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat from melted cheese component
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the high sodium content and saturated fat from the cheese melt warrant portion control; the lean chicken protein is a positive factor, but the overall sodium load makes regular consumption inadvisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken and Mushroom Melt Soup Meal is a Western-style fast-casual meal set sold at Subway outlets in Singapore, notably at Subway Funan. It consists of a warm chicken and mushroom soup topped or accompanied by melted cheese, served as a complete meal set alongside a bread roll or sandwich base. The soup component is a savoury broth enriched with tender pieces of chicken breast and sliced mushrooms, giving it an umami-forward, mildly creamy flavour profile. The melted cheese adds richness and a slightly indulgent texture contrast to the lighter soup base. The bread roll, typically a Subway-style white or Italian roll, contributes the majority of the carbohydrate content and provides a satisfying, filling element to the meal. Nutritionally, the meal set is a moderate-calorie option at approximately 118 kcal per 100g, with a reasonable protein content of 6.8g per 100g from the chicken. However, the sodium content is notably high at approximately 420 mg per 100g, translating to roughly 1890 mg per full 450g serving, which is a significant concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low-to-moderate), driven by the bread component, making portion awareness important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The meal is suitable for halal diets and contains common allergens including gluten and dairy. It is best consumed as an occasional meal rather than a daily staple due to its sodium load.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for a Subway Chicken and Mushroom Melt Soup Meal as a composite meal set. Values are estimated by compositing USDA SR Legacy data for chicken vegetable soup, cream of mushroom soup, and white bread roll in a 300g:150g ratio. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and variability in restaurant preparation.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chicken vegetable soup (FDC 173168), cream of mushroom soup (FDC 173175), and white bread roll (FDC 174924) in a 300g soup to 150g bread ratio. Actual values may differ based on Subway Singapore's specific recipe and portion sizes. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for the component foods used in this estimate. 6. GI is an analogous estimate (52, low category) based on weighted composite of chicken vegetable soup GI (~30) and white bread roll GI (~75) from the University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted for the cheese melt component. 7. Glycemic load per serving (12.1) is calculated on a 100g reference portion basis: (52 × 23.3g carbohydrate per 100g) / 100 = 12.1. 8. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite meal set containing protein, carbohydrate, and vegetable components. 9. Halal status is assumed based on Subway Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 10. The cuisine tag 'Sandwich' from the input has been retained in aliases; the primary cuisine classification is 'Western' consistent with Subway's brand identity.
